verdict_reasoning: TMED10 is a type I single-pass transmembrane protein (p24 family) with an extracellular GOLD domain. The NCBI summary explicitly states it localizes to the plasma membrane and Golgi cisternae — a classic dual-localization pattern. At the plasma membrane the extracellular GOLD domain is exposed on the outer leaflet, making it accessible to extracellular binders. The dominant compartment is the Golgi (COPI/COPII trafficking machinery), but a documented PM pool exists. This fits dual_localization: the protein carries its own TM anchor and cycles to the cell surface.
